title: Boundary
description: A cheat sheet for HashiCorp Boundary CLI commands and configurations.

commands.description: Start a Boundary dev server for local testing.
commands.scenario: Use when you want to quickly start a local Boundary server for development and testing.

commands.description: Authenticate to Boundary using password authentication.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to authenticate to Boundary with a username and password.

commands.description: List all targets in the global scope.
commands.scenario: Use to view all available targets within the global scope of Boundary.

commands.description: Establish a session to a target using its ID.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to connect to a specific target through Boundary.

commands.description: Create a new scope under the global scope.
commands.scenario: Use to organize resources by creating a new scope in Boundary.

commands.description: List all roles in the global scope.
commands.scenario: Use to view all roles available in the global scope of Boundary.

commands.description: Create a new role in the global scope.
commands.scenario: Use to define a new role with specific permissions in Boundary.

commands.description: List all accounts in the global scope.
commands.scenario: Use to see all user accounts within the global scope of Boundary.

commands.description: Create a new account in the global scope.
commands.scenario: Use to add a new user account to Boundary.

commands.description: Create a new target in the global scope.
commands.scenario: Use to define a new target resource in Boundary.

commands.description: List all sessions in the global scope.
commands.scenario: Use to monitor all active sessions within the global scope of Boundary.

commands.description: Cancel a specific session by its ID.
commands.scenario: Use to terminate an active session in Boundary.

commands.description: List all workers in the global scope.
commands.scenario: Use to view all workers available in the global scope of Boundary.

commands.description: Create a new worker in the global scope.
commands.scenario: Use to add a new worker to handle sessions in Boundary.

commands.description: List all authentication methods in the global scope.
commands.scenario: Use to see all available authentication methods in Boundary.

commands.description: Starts a local proxy listener to tunnel traffic to a target, allowing local applications to connect via localhost instead of using the Boundary CLI directly.
Provides detailed diagnostic information about a specific session, useful for troubleshooting connection failures or worker-side issues.
Configures an OIDC authentication method to enable enterprise Single Sign-On (SSO) integration.
Modifies the connection parameters (address or port) of an existing target without needing to recreate the entire object.
Filters the worker list to specifically view standard worker nodes, useful in large environments with mixed worker types.
